title: IAEA declares Iran non-compliant; secret enrichment site revealed
description: IAEA declared Iran non-compliant with nuclear obligations for the first time since 2005. Iran found to have enough 60%-enriched uranium for nine nuclear weapons and had established a secret enrichment facility.

title: Israel launches Operation Rising Lion - 200 jets hit 100+ targets in Iran
description: Israel launched a massive surprise air attack with 200 fighter jets striking over 100 targets across Iran. The operation assassinated IRGC head Hossein Salami, Armed Forces Chief Mohammed Bagheri, IRGC Air Force Commander Amir Ali Hajizadeh, and multiple nuclear scientists.
casualties: Multiple senior IRGC commanders and nuclear scientists killed

title: Iran and Houthis launch coordinated ballistic missile strikes on Israel
description: Iran and Houthi forces launched coordinated ballistic missile strikes on Israel, hitting Bat Yam, Rehovot, Kiryat Ekron, and Tel Aviv. 10 killed and approximately 200 injured in Israel.

title: US joins war - strikes Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan nuclear sites
description: The United States joined the conflict with B-2 stealth bombers and cruise missiles striking three major Iranian nuclear facilities. Iran retaliated by firing missiles at a US base in Qatar.

title: Ceasefire ends Twelve-Day War - Iran: ~1,000 killed; Israel: ~30 killed
description: A US-brokered ceasefire ended the Twelve-Day War. 16 killed in Iran and 4 in Israel during ceasefire violations in the first hours. Final toll: Iran 935-1,190 killed. Israel 28-30 killed.

title: Israel eliminates Houthi leadership in Yemen
description: Israeli forces conducted targeted strikes eliminating much of the Houthi leadership including their chief of staff. Houthis had been attacking Red Sea shipping and the Israeli port of Eilat.

title: E3 triggers snapback sanctions against Iran
description: UK, Germany, and France triggered snapback sanctions against Iran at the UN Security Council, reimposing international sanctions on Iran's nuclear program.

title: Massive Iranian protests erupt - largest since 1979 revolution
description: Nationwide protests erupted across 100+ Iranian cities following a sharp currency collapse. These became the largest protests since the 1979 revolution with demonstrators calling for regime change.

title: Iran's deadliest crackdown on protesters - thousands killed
description: Iranian security forces used live ammunition and DShK heavy machine guns against protesters. Thousands killed on Jan 8-9 alone. Internet shut down for two weeks. Gov says 3,117 killed; HRANA says 7,015; estimated up to 36,500.

title: US-Iran nuclear talks begin in Muscat, Oman
description: First round of indirect US-Iran nuclear negotiations held in Muscat, mediated by Omani FM. Three rounds followed through February.

title: IAEA discovers Iran hid enriched uranium - talks collapse
description: During Geneva talks, IAEA discovered Iran hid highly enriched uranium in an undisclosed underground facility. US envoys told Trump a deal was impossible. Presidential authorization for strikes given same day.

title: WAR BEGINS - US/Israel launch Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion
description: US and Israel launched massive simultaneous strikes. 2,000+ targets across 20+ Iranian cities. Khamenei's compound destroyed, killing the Supreme Leader and 40+ senior officials. Iran retaliated within hours hitting Tel Aviv and all US bases in the Gulf.

socialRefs.text: Massive explosions across Tehran, Isfahan, Qom. Anti-aircraft fire visible.

casualties: Khamenei killed, 40+ officials, hundreds of casualties

title: Iran confirms Khamenei dead - launches Operation True Promise IV
description: Iranian state media confirmed Khamenei's death, declared 40 days of mourning. IRGC launched 'heaviest offensive in Islamic Republic history.' Missile hit Beit Shemesh killing 9. All US Gulf bases targeted.
casualties: 9 killed in Beit Shemesh; 1 US KIA in Kuwait

title: 6 US service members killed at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait
description: Iranian missile strikes on Port Shuaiba killed 6 US service members and wounded 18. Highest US military casualty event of the conflict.

title: Hezbollah enters war - Israel strikes Beirut, kills intel chief
description: Hezbollah launched missiles and drones at Israel. Israel responded striking Beirut, killing Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussein Makled, destroying al-Manar TV. Ground incursion into southern Lebanon began with 91st Division.
casualties: Hezbollah intel chief killed; dozens killed; 85,000 displaced

title: 85-180 killed in Minab girls' school strike
description: Airstrike hit a girls' school in Minab, Iran. Initially 85 killed, later revised to ~180, mostly schoolgirls. Became the most controversial civilian casualty event of the war.

title: Iran strikes UAE - Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali, Abu Dhabi airports
description: Iran launched 165 ballistic missiles, 2 cruise missiles, and 541 drones at UAE. Targets included Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port, Abu Dhabi airports. Most intercepted but some impacts reported.

title: Israeli ground forces enter southern Lebanon
description: Israel's 91st Division began ground incursion into southern Lebanon. Lebanese PM banned Hezbollah military activities but Hezbollah defied the order. 85,000 displaced.

title: Iraqi militias launch 28 operations against US bases
description: Islamic Resistance in Iraq, Kataib Hezbollah, and allies claimed 28 operations with missiles and drones against US bases including Erbil and Camp Victoria. US/Israel struck back.

title: Strait of Hormuz blocked - 150+ vessels, 15M barrels/day halted
description: Strait of Hormuz effectively blocked. 150+ vessels anchored. 15 million barrels/day of oil blocked (20% of global supply). War-risk insurance canceled. Four vessels hit.

title: Qatar LNG production shut down after drone strikes
description: Qatar shut down LNG production after Iranian drone strikes. Qatar provides ~20% of global LNG. European natural gas futures surged 38%.

title: Iranian drones hit AWS data centers in Bahrain/UAE
description: Iranian drones struck Amazon Web Services data centers in Bahrain and UAE, taking them offline. US Embassy in Riyadh hit by drones. Washington warned all US citizens to evacuate the region.

title: US submarine sinks Iranian warship IRINS Dena
description: US submarine torpedoed and sank Iranian warship IRINS Dena off Sri Lanka. 101 of 180 sailors missing. US Senate defeated war powers resolution 47-53.

title: Qatari F-15s shoot down 2 Iranian jets attacking Al-Udeid
description: Two Iranian Su-24 jets attempted to strike Al-Udeid Air Base. Qatari F-15s intercepted and shot down both before they reached the base.

title: Hezbollah drone swarm attacks Ramat David airbase
description: Hezbollah launched large-scale drone swarm against Ramat David airbase and Golan Heights targets. Israeli air defenses engaged.

title: Day 6: New Israeli strikes on Tehran; Iran fires 9 waves at Israel
description: Israel launched new wave of strikes on Tehran military infrastructure. Iran responded with Khyber Shakan missiles at Tel Aviv and 9 waves targeting Jerusalem and southern Israel.

title: Iranian UAVs strike Azerbaijan (Nakhchivan) - new front
description: Iranian UAVs struck Azerbaijani territory in Nakhchivan for the first time, opening a new theater. First attack on non-belligerent Azerbaijani territory.

title: Iran declares readiness for 'long-term war'
description: Tehran declared readiness for long-term war and possible invasion preparations. Hegseth: US 'just getting started.' Trump offered Iranian forces 'immunity or guaranteed death.'

title: Iran back-channel to CIA via third-party intelligence service
description: Iran's intelligence ministry quietly reached out to CIA through another country's intelligence service to discuss terms. With Khamenei dead and fractured leadership, unclear who can commit Iran.

title: Global markets lose $3.2 trillion in 96 hours
description: $3.2T in global equity lost. KOSPI -12%, Pakistan KSE-100 -9.57% (largest-ever). Brent crude $82-84/bbl. European gas +38%. Gold touched $5,400. VLCC freight rates +94%.

title: B-2 stealth bombers strike Iranian underground facilities at Isfahan and Natanz
description: US B-2 Spirit stealth bombers deployed GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators against deeply buried Iranian nuclear enrichment facilities at Isfahan and Natanz. Pentagon reports multiple bunker-busting rounds penetrated hardened underground chambers. Secondary explosions observed at both sites.

title: Iran launches retaliatory ballistic missiles at US base in Bahrain
description: Iran's IRGC fired a salvo of Fateh-313 and Dezful medium-range ballistic missiles at Naval Support Activity Bahrain, home of the US 5th Fleet. Patriot and THAAD systems intercepted most projectiles but at least two missiles impacted within the base perimeter, wounding 12 US service members.

title: UN Security Council emergency session called over escalating conflict
description: The UN Security Council convened an emergency session after the Bahrain strike and B-2 bombing campaign. Secretary-General demanded an immediate ceasefire. Russia and China introduced a joint resolution condemning all parties; the US and UK vetoed. France abstained.

title: Red Cross requests humanitarian corridor for Tehran civilians
description: The International Committee of the Red Cross formally requested all belligerents establish a humanitarian corridor to allow civilian evacuation from Tehran's southern districts, which have suffered heavy bombardment. ICRC reports hospitals overwhelmed, medical supply shortages critical, and an estimated 500,000 internally displaced within the capital.

title: Massive cyber attack on Iranian banking system attributed to Israel
description: A coordinated cyber attack crippled Iran's central banking infrastructure, taking down the SHETAB interbank network, disabling ATMs nationwide, and freezing electronic transactions. Israeli Unit 8200 suspected. Millions of Iranians unable to access funds, exacerbating humanitarian crisis.

title: Oil prices surge past $90/barrel amid escalation fears
description: Brent crude surged past $90/barrel for the first time since the conflict began, hitting $91.40 intraday, as the B-2 strikes and Bahrain retaliation signaled deepening escalation. WTI crude reached $88.75. Goldman Sachs warned of $120 oil if Hormuz remains closed through April.

title: Iran announces capture of US drone over Persian Gulf
description: Iran's IRGC Navy announced the capture of a US RQ-4 Global Hawk surveillance drone, claiming it was downed by an indigenous surface-to-air missile system over the northern Persian Gulf. Iran broadcast footage of a largely intact drone airframe. Pentagon said it was 'reviewing the claim.'

title: Israel strikes Hezbollah command center in Beirut suburb
description: Israeli Air Force struck a Hezbollah underground command-and-control center in the Dahieh suburb of Beirut, using precision-guided munitions. Lebanese health ministry reported 23 killed including civilians in surrounding buildings. Israel claimed multiple senior Hezbollah military planners were eliminated.
casualties: 23 killed including civilians; senior Hezbollah commanders reported dead

title: Protests erupt in multiple US cities against war with Iran
description: Large-scale anti-war protests erupted in Washington D.C., New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, and San Francisco. Tens of thousands marched demanding an end to military operations against Iran. Clashes between protesters and police reported near the White House. Several Congress members joined the protests.
location: Washington D.C. and major US cities
casualties: Dozens arrested; minor injuries from clashes

title: Pentagon confirms 12 US service members wounded in Bahrain missile attack
description: The Pentagon confirmed that 12 US service members were wounded when Iranian ballistic missiles struck Naval Support Activity Bahrain. Three are in critical condition and were medically evacuated to Landstuhl Regional Medical Center in Germany. Defense Secretary called it 'an act of war against American forces that will not go unanswered.'

title: Israel strikes Tehran oil storage facility - massive fires near key refinery
description: Israeli warplanes targeted oil infrastructure in Tehran, hitting a major oil storage depot near a key refinery. Massive plumes of fire lit up the night sky. This marks a shift to targeting energy resources.

title: Trump signals possible ground troops in Iran - 'very good reason' needed
description: President Trump said US ground troops could 'possibly' be sent to Iran but there would have to be a 'very good reason.' NBC reports Trump has privately shown serious interest in ground deployment. Trump also demanded Iran's 'unconditional surrender.'

title: Iran fires new missile salvo at Tel Aviv - explosions reported across city
description: The Israeli military detected another round of Iranian missile fire headed towards Israel. A series of explosions were heard in Tel Aviv and across southern Israel. Missiles were also detected heading towards other parts of the country.

title: IRGC claims hits on oil tankers Prima and Louise P in Persian Gulf
description: The IRGC claimed it struck the oil tanker Prima with a drone in the Persian Gulf, and a US oil tanker Louise P with a drone in the Strait of Hormuz. At least 8 vessels have now been damaged since the blockade began.

title: Trump at Dover Air Force Base for dignified transfer of fallen US troops
description: President Trump visited Dover Air Force Base for the dignified transfer of remains of US service members killed in the Iran conflict. Six US servicemembers have died since the conflict began on February 28.

title: Israeli strike on Beirut hotel kills 4 - targeting IRGC commanders
description: Israeli jets struck a hotel in central Beirut, killing at least four people. Israel said it had targeted commanders from Iran's Revolutionary Guards who were present at the location. Lebanon's health ministry reports 217 killed since attacks began.

title: Iran claims capture of US soldiers - Trump administration denies
description: Ali Larijani, head of Iran's National Security Council, claimed Iran has captured US soldiers since the outbreak of war, suggesting the US was concealing captures. The Trump administration denied the reports.

title: US-Israel announce most intense bombing campaign yet entering week two
description: The US warned of a forthcoming bombing campaign that officials said would be the most intense yet in the weeklong conflict. Israel announced new waves of attacks in Tehran and Isfahan as operations enter their second week.
location: Multiple sites - Tehran and Isfahan, Iran

title: Iran names Mojtaba Khamenei as new Supreme Leader
description: Assembly of Experts elected Mojtaba Khamenei, son of slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as new Supreme Leader of Iran. The IRGC and all top leaders pledged allegiance to the new Supreme Leader.

title: Israel launches 'wide-scale' strikes on Tehran energy resources
description: Israeli military announced wide-scale strikes targeting Tehran energy resources and regime targets, marking a significant escalation in the campaign against Iranian infrastructure.

title: 8th US service member killed in Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia base
description: A US soldier died from injuries sustained during an Iranian missile attack on US troops stationed in Saudi Arabia on March 1, bringing the total US military deaths to eight since the conflict began.

title: Iran fires 500+ ballistic missiles and 2,000 drones since war began
description: Iranian military source confirmed Iran had launched over 500 ballistic and naval missiles and almost 2,000 drones since the beginning of the conflict. Approximately 40% were directed toward Israel and 60% toward US targets.

title: Iranian drone strikes British RAF base at Akrotiri, Cyprus
description: A Hezbollah-launched drone (Iranian-made) damaged the runway at the British military base at Akrotiri in Cyprus. French President Macron stated that an attack on Cyprus is an attack on all of Europe.

title: Qatar intercepted 17 Iranian ballistic missiles, 6 drones
description: Qatar's Ministry of Defence announced the successful interception of 17 ballistic missiles and 6 drones launched from Iran. No casualties or damage were reported.

title: Iranian drone damages Bahrain desalination plant
description: Bahrain Ministry of Interior confirmed a hostile Iranian drone struck the Salman Industrial City desalination plant. One person died from a fire caused by the strike.

title: Saudi Arabia: 2 killed in Iranian projectile strike on Al-Kharj
description: Two people were killed and 12 injured when a military projectile struck a residential facility in Al-Kharj near Riyadh. These are the first reported civilian deaths in Saudi Arabia since the conflict began.

title: Oil surges past $100/barrel as Strait of Hormuz effectively closed
description: Brent crude hit $119.50 intraday before settling around $103, marking the biggest weekly surge since 1983. The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to tanker traffic due to ongoing hostilities.

title: Trump demands Iran's 'unconditional surrender'
description: President Trump continued his demand for unconditional surrender from Iran, stating the war will continue 'a little while.' Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi rejected any ceasefire or talks with the Trump administration.

title: Dubai International Airport damaged by Iranian drone strikes
description: Dubai International Airport, one of the world's busiest, was damaged by drone strikes, temporarily halting all flights.

title: Swiss government declares US-Israel attacks violate international law
description: Swiss Defense Minister Martin Pfister said attacks on Iran constitute a violation of international law. He stated that the US, Israel, and Iran have all broken the prohibition on the use of force.

title: Iran's death toll surpasses 1,332 killed in 10 days
description: At least 1,332 people have been killed in Iran since the conflict began. Over 300 children have been hospitalized and more than 6,000 people have been wounded.

title: Kuwait child killed and 4 security personnel dead in Iranian attacks
description: Kuwait reported the death of one child in Iranian attacks. Four security personnel were also killed while on duty.

title: Pope Leo XIV calls for end to violence in Iran and Middle East
description: Pope Leo XIV urged an end to violence in Iran and the broader Middle East, calling for renewed dialogue between all parties involved in the conflict.

title: Senate war powers resolution fails — Trump authorized to continue Iran strikes
description: The US Senate voted down a war powers resolution that would have forced Trump to withdraw from military operations against Iran, giving the administration indefinite authorization to continue.

title: Trump: 'We've already won in many ways' — calls Mojtaba Khamenei a 'lightweight'
description: Trump told Times of Israel that the US has 'already won in many ways' and called the new Supreme Leader a 'lightweight.' Said he must have a role in choosing Iran's next leader and characterized the war as a 'short-term excursion.'

title: AI-generated fake satellite imagery of destroyed US base in Qatar goes viral
description: AFP confirmed an Iranian news outlet posted AI-generated fake satellite imagery showing a devastated US base in Qatar. Google SynthID watermark detected. Image garnered millions of views across platforms in multiple languages.

title: Blast outside synagogue in Liege, Belgium amid heightened tensions
description: An explosion occurred outside a synagogue in Liege, Belgium. Investigation underway. Occurring amid heightened global tensions from the Iran-Israel conflict.

title: IDF surprised Houthis have NOT joined Iran war — 'finger on trigger' but inactive
description: IDF sources expressed surprise that Yemen's Houthis have not attacked Israel despite expectations. Houthi leader said 'finger on the trigger, ready to respond at any moment' in a televised speech but has not acted. Analysts speculate US direct involvement is deterring Houthi entry.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei elected Iran's new Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts
description: Iran's Assembly of Experts elected Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the slain Supreme Leader, as the new Supreme Leader. IRGC reportedly pressured selection. IRGC immediately pledged allegiance.

title: Turkish NATO air defenses intercept Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace
description: NATO air defenses in Turkey shot down an Iranian ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace. This is the second interception over Turkey this week, raising Article 5 discussions.

title: Bahrain struck by Iranian missiles — 32 wounded in Sitra, 1 killed in Manama
description: Iranian missiles struck Bahrain, wounding 32 people including children in Sitra and killing a woman in a separate strike on a Manama residential area. This represents a significant escalation of Iranian attacks on Gulf states.

title: 8th US service member dies — Sgt. Benjamin Pennington from Saudi Arabia injuries
description: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, 26, died from injuries sustained during Iranian strikes on a US base in Saudi Arabia. He is the 8th US service member killed in the conflict.

title: House votes against war powers resolution 212-219
description: The US House of Representatives voted 212-219 against a war powers resolution, joining the Senate's 47-53 defeat. No Congressional check remains on Iran operations.

title: US-Israeli airstrike kills 40+ in Tehran residential buildings
description: An attack on residential buildings in eastern Tehran killed at least 40 people. Earlier attacks on oil facilities blanketed the capital in toxic smoke. Iran's deputy health minister confirmed over 1,255 killed and 12,000 wounded in 11 days of war.

title: US-Israeli airstrike hits residential building in Arak — 5 killed
description: Five people were killed and several injured in a US-Israeli air strike that hit a residential building in the city of Arak in western Iran, according to Iranian state news agency ISNA.

title: Isfahan historical sites damaged — Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Shah Mosque hit
description: Strikes on Isfahan on March 9-10 damaged multiple UNESCO World Heritage sites including Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Chehel Sotoun palace, Ali Qapu, Shah Mosque, and Teymouri Hall. International cultural organizations condemned the destruction of irreplaceable heritage.

title: Hegseth warns Day 12 will be 'most intense day of strikes' against Iran
description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warned that Tuesday (Day 12) will see the most intense day of strikes against Iran since the start of the war, signaling a major escalation of the aerial campaign.

title: Iran threatens 'eye for an eye' retaliation if US-Israel hits infrastructure
description: Iran vowed an 'eye for an eye' response if the US and Israel continue to strike Iranian oil and energy infrastructure. A senior Iranian official warned that Israel's attacks on oil and fuel depots have pushed the war into a 'new phase' and threatened retaliatory strikes on energy infrastructure.

title: China, Russia, France contact Iran regarding ceasefire
description: Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi confirmed that several countries including China, Russia, and France have contacted Iran regarding a possible ceasefire. However, Iran's Foreign Ministry stated it has no interest in negotiations while under attack.

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts Iranian drone east of al-Kharj
description: Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry confirmed its military intercepted and destroyed an Iranian drone east of al-Kharj governorate, south of the capital Riyadh. Saudi Arabia has been a repeated target of Iranian retaliatory strikes due to hosting US military bases.

title: Iranian drone attack targets Haifa oil refinery in Israel
description: Iran's army issued a statement saying it launched a drone attack targeting an Israeli oil and gas refinery and fuel tanks in Haifa. Israeli air defenses engaged multiple incoming drones. Damage assessment ongoing.

title: Lebanese priest killed by Israeli tank fire in Qlayaa
description: A Lebanese Maronite Catholic priest was killed by Israeli tank fire in the Christian village of Qlayaa in southern Lebanon. Reports indicate he had refused an Israeli order for forced evacuation of the town. The incident drew widespread international condemnation.

title: Iran cluster munition strike kills 2 in central Israel — 300 missiles fired total
description: IDF confirmed Iran has fired approximately 300 missiles at Israel since the war began, with about half carrying cluster bomb warheads banned under the 2008 Convention on Cluster Munitions. A cluster munition strike on central Israel killed 2 people and seriously wounded 1, hitting 6 sites across the region. 186 total attack waves have been launched against Israel.

title: US destroys 16 Iranian minelayers near Strait of Hormuz
description: US Navy destroyed 16 Iranian naval minelayers operating near the Strait of Hormuz. A second IRGC 'Shahid Soleimani'-class warship was also destroyed at Bandar Abbas port. CENTCOM confirmed 5,000+ total targets struck since Feb 28.

title: Oil prices crash 11-12% after Trump says war ending 'very soon'
description: Brent crude crashed 11.28% to $87.80/barrel and WTI fell 11.94% to $83.45 after Trump told Republican lawmakers the war could end 'pretty quickly' and 'very soon.' Biggest single-day oil drop since conflict began. Prices had peaked at $119.50 intraday.

title: China bans rare earth exports for military use amid Iran war
description: China barred export of rare earth elements for military use, a move that could significantly impact US weapons replenishment during the conflict. Xi Jinping is prioritizing US relations over Iran, with a summit with Trump planned in Beijing.

title: Iran launches 37th wave with super-heavy Khoramshahr missiles
description: Iran launched its '37th wave' of attacks deploying super-heavy Khoramshahr ballistic missiles — the largest in its arsenal with 2,000+ km range and 1,800 kg warhead. Targets included Israel, US bases in the Gulf, and Saudi Arabia. IDF reported multiple interceptions but debris caused casualties.

title: 3 more ships attacked near Hormuz — total 14 vessels damaged/sunk
description: Three additional commercial vessels were attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Day 12, bringing the total to 14 ships damaged or sunk since the war began. Shipping traffic down 95% from pre-war levels. Over 320 vessels at anchor.

title: Lebanon cabinet votes 22-2 to declare Hezbollah military activities illegal
description: Lebanese cabinet voted 22-2 to declare Hezbollah's military activities illegal — a historic move to distance from the Iran-backed militia. Hezbollah's political wing remains legal. The vote came under intense Israeli military pressure with 486 killed and 700,000 displaced in Lebanon.

title: IEA approves record 400M barrel emergency oil reserve release
description: The International Energy Agency approved the release of 400 million barrels from strategic oil reserves over 60 days — the largest coordinated drawdown in IEA history, surpassing the 2022 release of 182M barrels. Oil rebounded 3.4% to Brent $90.77 and WTI $86.01.

title: Spain permanently withdraws ambassador to Israel over Iran war opposition
description: Spain permanently withdrew its ambassador to Israel, escalating a diplomatic rift. PM Sanchez condemned the US-Israel attack on Iran as 'unjustifiable' and refused to allow US to use Spanish military bases for Iran operations. Trump accused Spain of being 'unfriendly' and threatened trade cuts.

title: FBI warns California of potential Iran drone attack on West Coast
description: FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force warned California police that Iran 'aspired to conduct a surprise attack using drones from an unidentified vessel off the US coast.' Multiple officials say threat is not currently deemed credible. Oscars security elevated for March 15.

title: Greece caps fuel and food profit margins for 3 months amid Iran war price surge
description: Greece imposed 3-month profit margin caps on fuel (12-cent/liter retail markup limit) and food to prevent profiteering from Iran war-driven price surges. Germany limited gas station price changes to once daily. Italy reviewing extra VAT use to cushion consumers.

title: Iran demands guarantee of no future US-Israel attacks as ceasefire condition
description: Iran told regional intermediaries it will only agree to a ceasefire if the US guarantees neither America nor Israel will attack Iran again. Tehran also demands compensation payments and right to full nuclear fuel cycle. President Pezeshkian laid out conditions publicly.

title: Six vessels attacked in Gulf and Strait of Hormuz
description: Six commercial vessels attacked in the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz. Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree hit by two projectiles, engine room fire, 3 crew missing. Japan-flagged ONE Majesty sustained minor damage 25nm NW of Ras Al Khaimah. Iranian explosive boats attacked two fuel tankers (Safesea Vishnu, Zefyros) in Iraqi waters, killing 1 crew member. Total ships struck since war began: at least 16.

title: Iran threatens US tech giant offices across Middle East
description: Iran's Tasnim news agency published a target list of US tech company offices in Israel and Gulf states, including Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Palantir, IBM, and Oracle. IRGC described them as 'enemy technology infrastructure' and warned of expanding 'infrastructure warfare' in retaliation for strikes on Iranian banks.

title: Iran-linked hackers attack US medical tech company Stryker
description: A pro-Iran hacking group claimed responsibility for a cyberattack on Stryker, a US medical technology company. This is the first significant instance of Iran hacking an American company since the war began. The group said it was 'in retaliation for the brutal attack on the Minab school.'

title: Israel strikes apartment building in central Beirut
description: Israeli airstrike hit an apartment in the Aisha Bakkar area of central Beirut, a densely populated neighborhood near one of the city's biggest shopping malls. AFPTV captured a fireball erupting on the 7th-8th floors. Separately, a strike on Beirut's seafront at Ramlet al-Baida killed at least 7 people. Lebanon death toll: 634 killed, 1,586 injured, 816K displaced.

title: Hezbollah rocket strikes Moshav Haniel in central Israel
description: A Hezbollah rocket struck Moshav Haniel in central Israel, causing massive damage to a home and creating a crater. The blast virtually destroyed the building except for the reinforced safe room. A woman and her carer reached the safe room during the 90-second siren warning.

title: Hezbollah launches largest barrage — 200 rockets and 20 drones at northern Israel
description: Hezbollah launched approximately 200 rockets and 20 drones at northern Israel in its largest attack since the war began. The onslaught sent hundreds of thousands of Israelis to shelters. Iran's IRGC described it as a 'joint and integrated operation' with Hezbollah, claiming more than 50 targets struck across Israel. Targets included Nahariya, Beit Lid military base, Glilot (Unit 8200 HQ), Meron base, and Atlit naval base.

title: Iran fires advanced ballistic missiles at Tel Aviv and Haifa — 'most intense operation' of the war
description: Iran launched its 'most intense operation since the beginning of the war' according to state media, firing advanced ballistic missiles toward Tel Aviv and Haifa. The IRGC described it as a joint operation with Hezbollah lasting five hours, claiming to have struck over 50 targets across Israel.

title: Iraq shuts down oil port operations after tanker attacks
description: Iraq shut down oil port operations following deadly attacks on two foreign oil tankers in Iraqi territorial waters. The closure affects a major export route for Iraqi crude oil, adding further pressure to global energy markets already strained by the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

title: Iran sets 3 conditions for peace — demands recognition, reparations, and security guarantees
description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian outlined three conditions for peace negotiations: recognition of Tehran's sovereign rights, war reparations for damage caused by US-Israeli strikes, and binding guarantees against future military aggression. This is the first formal articulation of Iran's demands for ending hostilities.

title: Kuwait shoots down 8 Iranian drones — Gulf states under sustained attack
description: Kuwait shot down 8 Iranian drones as Gulf states continued to face attacks on Day 13 of the conflict. The UAE was responding to Iranian missiles, and Qatar intercepted a missile attack. The ongoing attacks on Gulf states that host US military bases represent a major regional escalation.

title: Russia providing tactical drone advice to Iran — new level of military support
description: Reports indicate Russia is giving Iran specific tactical advice on drone tactics, helping Tehran hit US and Gulf nation targets. This represents a new level of support beyond previously reported general military assistance, escalating the conflict's geopolitical dimensions.

title: Israel threatens Lebanon national infrastructure over Hezbollah attacks
description: Israel sent a warning to Lebanon's government through the US and Western countries that if Beirut doesn't rein in Hezbollah, Israel will attack national infrastructure. Defense Minister Katz threatened to 'take territory' unless Lebanon prevents Hezbollah attacks.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei issues first statement as Supreme Leader
description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first statement via Telegram, calling for national unity, continued Hormuz closure, and military resistance. He said Iran is studying 'opening of other fronts.' His physical condition remains uncertain — reportedly suffered a fractured foot and minor facial injuries from the Feb 28 strike.

title: Netanyahu holds first wartime press conference — says Israel 'crushing' Iran
description: Netanyahu held his first press conference since the war began, claiming Israel is 'crushing' Iran and Hezbollah. He called Mojtaba Khamenei an 'IRGC puppet' who 'cannot show his face in public.' He addressed Iranian people directly: 'Coming days will bring opportunity to free yourselves, be ready.'

title: Brent crude surges past $100/barrel — first time since 2022
description: Brent crude surged 9.2% to $100.46/barrel, crossing $100 for the first time since 2022. WTI also jumped. The spike was driven by continued Hormuz closure, Iraq port shutdowns, and Iranian attacks on shipping. IEA agreed to release a record 400 million barrels from emergency reserves, with the US contributing 172 million from the SPR.

title: Israel expands Lebanon operations past Litani to Zahrani River
description: Israel expanded military operations in Lebanon, issuing new evacuation orders past the Litani River to the Zahrani River. Overnight strikes hit Beirut's Corniche seafront (a designated evacuation area), killing 8+. IDF also struck Beqaa Valley targets. Hezbollah retook positions south of the Litani that the Lebanese Armed Forces had taken over under the Nov 2024 ceasefire.

title: UNHCR: 3.2 million displaced across Iran
description: UNHCR reported 3.2 million people have been temporarily displaced across Iran since the conflict began, affecting 600,000-1 million households. Additionally 1.65 million refugees already in Iran (750,000 Afghans) are at further risk. Iran's infrastructure damage includes ~20,000 civilian buildings, 77 healthcare facilities, and 65 schools.

title: US stock markets hit 2026 lows — Dow drops 739 points
description: US stock markets fell to 2026 closing lows as the war's economic impact deepened. Dow Jones dropped 739 points (-1.56%) to 46,677. S&P 500 fell 1.52% to 6,672. Nasdaq dropped 1.78% to 22,311. Markets reacted to oil crossing $100 and growing concerns about a prolonged conflict.

title: CENTCOM: 6,000 targets struck in Iran since Feb 28
description: US Central Command reported approximately 6,000 targets have been struck across Iran since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28. Over 90 Iranian vessels including 30+ mine-laying ships have been damaged or destroyed. The US also destroyed 16 Iranian minelayer ships near the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Trump says war ending 'soon' — Israel says 'no time limit'
description: Trump told media the war would end 'soon' because 'there's practically nothing left to target,' adding 'Anytime I want it to end, it will end.' However, Israeli Defence Minister Katz contradicted this, stating the operation 'will continue without any time limit, as long as required.' The divergence signals potential US-Israel friction over war duration.

title: US KC-135 Stratotanker crashes in western Iraq near Turaibil
description: A US Boeing KC-135 Stratotanker aerial refueling tanker crashed in western Iraq near the Turaibil border crossing with Jordan. CENTCOM confirmed 5 crew aboard and stated the crash was NOT due to hostile fire. This is the 4th manned US aircraft lost this month during the Iran campaign.

title: French soldier killed in drone strike near Makhmour, Iraq — first non-US NATO casualty
description: French Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion of the 7th Alpine Chasseurs Battalion was killed in a drone strike at a Peshmerga/coalition base near Makhmour in Iraq's Erbil region. Five other French soldiers were wounded. This marks the first NATO casualty outside the US in the Iran war. President Macron called it 'unacceptable.'

casualties.note: WO Arnaud Frion killed, 5 French soldiers wounded

title: Iran launches 'most intense operation' — Ghadr, Emad, Fattah missiles at Israel and Gulf bases
description: Iran fired Ghadr, Emad, Kheybar Shekan, and Fattah missiles at Tel Aviv, Haifa, Israeli military intelligence HQ, Haifa naval base, radar systems, and US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. Iran described it as their 'most intense operation' of the war. Israeli media reported all missiles intercepted.

title: Two oil tankers hit in Iraqi territorial waters near Basra
description: Two oil tankers were struck in Iraqi territorial waters near Basra, marking the first oil-related strike inside Iraqi waters during the conflict. The attack threatens to further disrupt oil exports from Iraq's southern terminals.

title: Iran's President Pezeshkian sets 3 conditions for peace
description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian outlined three conditions to end the war: (1) recognition of Iran's legitimate rights, (2) guarantees against future attacks on Iran, and (3) compensation and reparations for war damages. The conditions represent the first formal Iranian peace framework since the war began.

title: WHO warns of toxic 'black rain' over Tehran from oil depot strikes
description: The WHO warned that Israeli/US strikes on Iranian oil depots are causing toxic 'black rain' containing benzene and toluene — known carcinogens — to fall over Tehran and surrounding areas. The environmental crisis adds a humanitarian dimension to the ongoing air campaign against Iranian energy infrastructure.

title: KC-135 crew deaths confirmed — all 6 killed in Iraq crash
description: CENTCOM confirmed all 6 crew members of the KC-135 Stratotanker that crashed near Turaibil, Iraq on March 12 were killed. Reports indicate a possible mid-air collision with another KC-135, which landed safely at Ben Gurion Airport with tail fin damage. Total US military deaths since Feb 28 now stands at 13 service members.

casualties.note: All 6 crew members confirmed dead. Total US military deaths at 13.

title: NATO intercepts 3rd Iranian missile over Turkey near Adana
description: NATO air defenses intercepted a third Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace near Adana, home to Incirlik Air Base. Residents were woken by sirens at 3:25 AM local time. Turkey confirmed the interception and condemned the violation of its airspace.

title: Iranian missile strikes Zarzir in northern Israel — 50+ wounded
description: An Iranian missile struck the town of Zarzir in northern Israel, moderately wounding a woman and injuring over 50 people from shrapnel and flying glass. A teen girl was also killed running to a shelter during the attack. The strike was part of continued Iranian missile salvos targeting northern Israel.

casualties.note: 1 woman moderately wounded, teen girl killed running to shelter, 50+ injured by glass/shrapnel

title: Explosion at Al-Quds Day rally in Tehran — 1 killed
description: An explosion rocked an Al-Quds Day solidarity march in Tehran, killing at least 1 person as thousands participated in the annual rally. The cause of the explosion remains unclear. Al-Quds Day rallies are held annually in support of Palestinians.

casualties.note: At least 1 killed at rally, cause under investigation

title: Michigan synagogue attack — vehicle rams Temple Israel near Detroit
description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali rammed a vehicle into Temple Israel synagogue near Detroit, Michigan. The suspect's relatives were reportedly killed in an Israeli strike. The attack represents a domestic spillover of the Iran-Israel conflict onto US soil.

title: Netanyahu first press conference since war — says Israel is 'crushing' Iran
description: In his first press conference since the war began, Netanyahu declared Israel is 'crushing' Iran and mocked new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei for not showing his face publicly. He addressed the Iranian people directly, saying the 'coming days will bring opportunity to free yourselves.'

title: Treasury waives Russia oil sanctions for one month to ease supply crunch
description: The US Treasury Department waived sanctions on Russian oil exports for one month to free stranded tankers and ease the global supply crunch caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The move signals the severity of the energy crisis as oil prices remain above $100/barrel.

title: 11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrate Israeli air defense — 2 killed
description: Eleven Iranian cluster missiles penetrated Israeli air defenses, with one dropping approximately 70 cluster bombs over central Israel. Two Israelis were killed in the attack. The use of cluster munitions — banned under the 2008 Convention on Cluster Munitions — marks a significant escalation in Iran's targeting of Israeli population centers.

casualties.note: 2 Israelis killed by cluster munitions in central Israel

title: All 6 KC-135 crew confirmed dead in Iraq crash — US death toll reaches 13
description: Pentagon confirms all 6 crew aboard US Air Force KC-135 tanker killed in western Iraq crash. Possible mid-air collision with second tanker that landed at Ben Gurion with tail fin damage. Total US service members killed: 13. 4th manned US aircraft lost this month.

casualties.note: All 6 crew members killed. 4th manned US aircraft lost.

title: 2,200 Marines (31st MEU) deployed to Middle East from Japan
description: 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit with 2,200 Marines on USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group (USS Tripoli, USS San Diego, USS New Orleans) plus ~20 F-35Bs ordered from Japan to Middle East. Pentagon does not rule out ground operations.

title: Israel destroys Zrarieh Bridge in Lebanon — first civilian infrastructure strike
description: Israel destroys the Zrarieh Bridge over the Litani River, the first acknowledged strike on civilian infrastructure in Lebanon. Leaflets dropped over Beirut warn of 'Gaza-scale devastation.' 25+ killed in Beirut and southern Lebanon in expanded strike campaign.

casualties.note: 25+ killed across Beirut, southern and eastern Lebanon

title: Hegseth: 15,000+ targets struck — highest volume strikes yet over Iran
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth announces 'highest volume of strikes' over Iran, with 15,000+ enemy targets hit (1,000+/day). Claims Iran missile capacity down 90%, drone capacity down 95%. Over 60 Iranian ships and 30 minelayers eliminated. Won't rule out ground forces to secure enriched uranium.

title: US waives Russian oil sanctions for 30 days amid energy crisis
description: US Treasury waives Russian oil sanctions for 30 days (until April 11) to free stranded tankers and ease global supply crunch. EU condemns the move. Macron says Hormuz closure 'in no way justifies' lifting Russia sanctions. Unprecedented geopolitical realignment driven by energy crisis.
